subtitle: The 8-kilometer Canadian D-Day beach at Courseulles, Bernières, and Saint-Aubin, where ~340 Canadians were killed on 6 June 1944 and where two communes now run a supervised summer swimming beach in front of the bunkers.
spike_statement: Canada's D-Day beach: ~340 Canadians killed on 6 June 1944, now a lifeguarded summer resort beach in front of the same German bunkers.
